name: Master Hunter
concept: You feed the tribe and guard its edges, running the wild with a bond-wolf at your side and a bow that does not miss. The hunt is not sport to you; it is life and death for every mouth in the Holt. You know the wood the way you know your own heartbeat, and the wolf that runs with you knows your soul.
duty: The tribe eats because you hunt, and sleeps because you watch. The wild is your charge, and your bond-wolf your other half.

look.label: Look
options: scarred from quarry that fought back
moving like they belong to the wood

look.label: Wolf
options: a young wolf you are still learning

look.label: Eyes
options: patient and cold on the hunt

gear.label: Bow
options: a short bow made for the deep wood (d6)

gear.label: Hunt-kit
options: a whistle only your wolf answers

gear.label: Bond
options: a token you and your wolf share
a debt to the pack that raised your wolf
the pelt of the first great beast you took

moves.name: The Hunt
moves.trigger: When you stalk and strike your quarry — beast or foe — from cover and at the right moment.

results.success: Your shot or strike lands, and you are still unseen.
results.failure: The quarry breaks, or the strike goes wide, and now it knows you're there.
advantage: Hold 1 Quarry (spend: a killing shot, clean and quick / stay hidden even after the strike / drive the quarry where you want it / bring down enough to feed the tribe well).
You read the quarry — where it will run, what it guards, what it fears.
threat: You loose your last good arrows, or your position is given away.
The hunt runs long and hard, drawing you far from the tribe or into worse country.
results.triumph: A clean kill that will be a song at the fire, and meat for many days.
results.despair: The hunted turns hunter, and now it is stalking you.

hold.name: Quarry
spend: A killing shot or strike, clean and quick.
Stay hidden even after you strike.
Drive the quarry exactly where you want it.
Bring down enough to feed the tribe well.

moves.name: Wolf-Friend
moves.trigger: Because a bond-wolf runs at your side, soul to soul:
moves.text: Your wolf hunts, guards, and fights with you as one; take a boost die when you act together, and it will do things for you no wild beast would. But the bond runs both ways: your wolf is not a tool but kin, and its death would wound your very soul. Where you go, you weigh whether the wolf should follow.

moves.name: Track
moves.trigger: When you read trail, spoor, and sign to follow something, or to know what passed.

results.success: The trail speaks: you know what passed, when, and where it went.
results.failure: The sign is cold or crossed, and following it wrong will cost you.
advantage: Learn the number, health, or mood of what you track.
Find where they can be caught, or avoided.
threat: Tracking them, you left a trail of your own.
results.triumph: You could follow them to the ends of the wood.
results.despair: The trail was laid for you to follow.

moves.name: Bowmaster
moves.trigger: When you take a shot that only a master could make.

results.success: The arrow flies true and does exactly what you needed.
results.failure: The shot fails, and you've spent it and shown your hand.
advantage: Keep the range: they can't close before you loose again.
The shot does more — disarms, pins, drops a leader.
threat: Your last good arrow is gone.
The shot gives away your position.
results.triumph: An impossible shot that decides the moment.
results.despair: You miss the one shot that mattered.

moves.name: Run with the Pack
moves.trigger: When you pursue, flee, or range far across the wild, alone or with wolf and hunters.

results.success: You cover the ground, tireless, and get where you must be.
results.failure: The wild wears you down, or turns you from your path.
advantage: Bring the whole hunting party along at your pace.
Arrive unseen, or fresh enough to fight.
threat: The running costs you: a setback until you rest.
You range so far you're cut off from help.
results.triumph: You run down what fled, or outrun what chased, without fail.
results.despair: You outrun your own strength, or your pack, at the worst moment.

bonds: ____ has hunted at my side since we were cubs; we barely need words.
____ thinks I take too many risks in the wild, and worries after me.
I am teaching ____ the ways of the hunt, and the wolf has taken to them.
____ once saved my wolf's life, and I owe them a debt I feel in my soul.
My wolf trusts ____ almost as it trusts me, and that is no small thing.
communityTie: The Hollow-Oak Holt eats because I hunt; ____ makes sure I don't range so far I forget to come home.
When something dangerous nears the wood's edge, I know it first; ____ is the one I send word to.
My wolf is half of me and part of the tribe; ____ understands what it would mean to lose it.

basic: Take a move from another playbook.
Take a move from another playbook.
Increase your maximum held Quarry by 1.
Deepen the wolf-bond: your wolf and you can act as one even apart, sharing senses at need.
Train a move you lean on: it upgrades 2 dice to proficiency (never more than its stat).
Learn a range: name a stretch of wild you know so well nothing there can ambush you.
advanced: Raise one stat by 1 (max 5). Rare, and only at a true turning point.
Run with a pack: your bond-wolf brings its kin, and you can call on wolves at need.
Claim the hunting grounds: found a Bastion (a hunt-lodge and secured range) that feeds the tribe through any winter.
